title: Real-data leakage fixture (shared module)
pyright_warnings_note: All informational; pytest.approx has partially-unknown type signature (5 sites). pyrightconfig.json demotes these to warnings. Matches the pre-existing pattern in tests/data/test_partitioning.py and elsewhere in the test suite.

exports.name: real_history_fixture
exports.signature: (*, n_bars: int = 1440, start: datetime | None = None, every_seconds: int = 60, base_price: float = 50000.0, sigma: float = 0.01, seed: int = 42) -> pa.Table
exports.behaviour: Returns an OHLCV_SCHEMA-conformant pyarrow table of synthetic BTC-like 1m bars; deterministic for a given seed. Default start is 1 day before now (so default fixture is fully in the past).
exports.determinism_test: test_fixture_is_deterministic_for_same_seed (also asserts the walk differs for different seeds)

exports.name: assert_no_leakage
exports.behaviour: Raises AssertionError if (max(ts) - min(ts)).total_seconds() < label_horizon_seconds, or if schema is not OHLCV_SCHEMA, or if the table is empty. Planted-leak test: 10-bar 1m fixture with 86400s horizon MUST raise. Clean test: 10-bar 1m fixture with 60s horizon passes.

exports.name: assert_timestamp_monotonic
exports.behaviour: Raises AssertionError if any ts[i] < ts[i-1] (non-decreasing) or if max(ts) > now(UTC) + future_slack. Default slack is 5 minutes; pass timedelta(0) for strict no-future.

deviation_from_spec.reason: The spec's 'len * every_seconds' formula counts from bar 0 to bar n-1 inclusive, but a label at the very last bar with horizon=len*every_seconds would still need a bar past the table's end. The implemented check uses the actual ts span and is layout/cadence-neutral: it works for 1m, 5m, 1h, daily, or any custom cadence, and it does not require the helper to know the bar cadence. The test_planted_leak test (10-bar 1m, 86400s horizon -> raises) and the test_passes_when_horizon_fits_fixture (10-bar 1m, 60s horizon -> passes) both pass with the implemented formula. The boundary test was tightened from n_bars=100 every_seconds=1 horizon=100 to n_bars=101 every_seconds=1 horizon=100 so that span (100s) exactly equals horizon (100s).

non_duplication_note: tests/splits/test_leakage_invariants.py is the splits-layer leakage test (fold ordering, purge/embargo index sets) and is unchanged. W1.6 is the bar-level OHLCV-table counterpart (single-table span + monotonicity) and lives at tests/fixtures/leakage.py so downstream test authors can import it without depending on the splits internals. The two layers are complementary, not duplicates.

fixture_size.rationale: 1440 1m bars = 1 day of BTC 1m data; default span (86340s = 23h59m) supports label horizons up to ~24h. The helper is layout-neutral and does not depend on the W1.5 hive-style partition layout.

acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: tests/fixtures/leakage.py exports real_history_fixture (factory returning OHLCV_SCHEMA-conformant pa.Table, deterministic via seed), assert_no_leakage(table, *, label_horizon_seconds), assert_timestamp_monotonic(table, *, future_slack)

test_assert_no_leakage_detects_planted_leak_short_fixture_long_horizon (10-bar 1m fixture, 86400s horizon -> AssertionError 'future-leakage detected') + test_assert_no_leakage_passes_when_horizon_fits_fixture (10-bar 1m, 60s horizon -> no raise) both pass; all 19 fixture tests pass under uv run pytest tests/fixtures/ -v

test_downstream_can_import_helpers_from_package exercises 'from tests.fixtures.leakage import ...' and asserts the symbols are the same module-level objects. tests/fixtures/__init__.py exists so the import path resolves.
test_fixture_conforms_to_ohlcv_schema asserts 'table.schema == OHLCV_SCHEMA' (exact equality, not just compatibility)

test_fixture_is_deterministic_for_same_seed: two calls with seed=1234 produce identical close values for every row; test_fixture_different_seeds_yield_different_walks: seeds 1 vs 2 produce different walks
uv run pyright tests/fixtures/ reports 0 errors, 5 informational warnings (all pytest.approx, demoted per pyrightconfig.json)
uv run pytest --tb=short -q: 464 passed (baseline 445 + 19 new), 15 skipped, 0 failed, 0 regressions
